      Milestones & Moments · preview
      ▾
      Moments that made
      this real.
      A completed profile is one side of the story. Milestones & Moments is the other — the living record of appreciations, photos, and milestones you build over the life of your partnership. A few entries from this couple’s record are below.
      Milestones & Moments is a FUSE Align subscriber feature. It grows with you.
      Section 06
      Who this profile is designed for
      ▾
      The two of you
      A living record of where you are, what you’ve named, and what’s still worth the conversation.
      Couples therapy intake
      Arrive with structural data already gathered. Skip the first six sessions of discovery work and start with what matters.
      Pre-marital counseling
      Hand this to your pastor, priest, or counselor before the first session. The categories they would have asked about, already answered.
      “Is this working?” conversations
      When the hard season hits, open the profile together. Compare this year’s scores to last year’s. Decide what’s still true.
      A longitudinal record
      Five years of continuous Align subscription equals five years of measured alignment. You cannot reconstruct this retroactively.
      This is a sample profile. Every Align subscriber generates their own, updated continuously as answers evolve and new categories complete. Exportable at any time.

      Your completed categories
      Focus now
      Strong
      Relationship Structure came back at 82%. Your foundation on monogamy and emotional exclusivity is real — not an assumption.
      The conversation worth having next:
      Money.
      68% — meaningful gap. Alex ranked financial transparency Non-Negotiable; you ranked it Strong Preference. The asymmetry in flexibility is the actual finding, not the score. The conversation worth having is what “transparency” specifically means to each of you, before the unspoken expectation becomes the disagreement.
      Three places to start the conversation
      —What does “full transparency” actually look like day-to-day? A shared dashboard, monthly sit-downs, or asking before any purchase over a certain amount?
      —Where did each of you learn your instinct around money — parents, past partners, or a lesson you lived through?
      —What’s the number above which you’d want to talk before spending?

      Preview · Month 4
      Week 14 · Tuesday
      How are you doing
      this week?

      Relationship pulse this week
      StrugglingThriving
      Question of the Week
      “What’s one thing your partner did this week that you want them to know you noticed?”
      Has anything shifted?
      You both ranked Money as a top-3 priority. Has your thinking about it changed at all this week?
      Since last week
      Your pulse has been between 3 and 4 for the past 3 weeks. That’s steady ground. Alex’s pulse has been between 4 and 5. Worth a check-in conversation about what’s working for them.
      About this preview
      The Weekly Check-In is part of the Month 4 product update. It’s shown here as a preview so you can see how your profile stays alive between full category work. Final version will include pulse history charts, shared vs. private modes, and partner notifications.
      Category
      Sensitive Category
      There are no right answers here — only honest ones. Answer based on where you actually stand.
      01
      Category 01
      Money & Financial Philosophy
      How you think about money, debt, risk appetite, and financial transparency.
      PRIVATE
      Your answer is sealed until your partner completes theirs. No peeking. No anchoring.
      Core Question
      “What role does money play in your sense of security — and how much financial transparency do you need from a partner?”
      One person’s example
      This is one person’s perspective, not a template. Your answer should reflect your own experience, not echo this one.
      How flexible are you on this?
      Non-Negotiable
      Essential to your long-term happiness. Misalignment here would prevent the relationship from moving forward.
      Strong Preference
      Important to you. Adjustment would require meaningful compromise and intentional discussion.
      Flexible
      You have a preference, but you could adapt without long-term resentment.
      Unsure
      You have not yet formed a clear position.
      Priority RankYour 1 free rank
      Rank this category against the other 15 in your profile. The category you rank #1 is the single most important dimension of the shared life you want to build. Each number should be used only once. Two partners can share identical answers and still be misaligned if they disagree on what matters most.
      Where does this category rank in your 16 core categories?
      Priority Rank · Align feature
      Ranking is where priorities become visible.
      Two partners can share identical answers and still be misaligned if they disagree on what matters most. Priority ranking is the mechanism designed to surface those hidden conflicts — and it’s an Align feature. Unlock it to rank this category against all 16 in your profile.
      Just this category$9.99 one-time · locked at purchase
      Align (all 16 + ongoing)$199/yr · evolves with you

      Where You Stand
      ⓘ
      FUSE Align provides relationship intelligence and conversation tools. It is not therapy or medical advice. If you or your partner are in crisis, contact a professional immediately.
      Guided Conversation
      Financial Transparency Gap
      A structured
      conversation guide.
      Each prompt is specific to what your alignment data revealed — not generic advice.
      Prompt 01 of 04
      “When you imagine full financial transparency in a relationship, what specifically do you picture — and what does having that tell you about trust?”
      Answer independently first, then share. The goal is to understand where each position comes from.
      Conversation Ground Rules
      —
      No interrupting while the other person is speaking
      —
      Speak from your own experience. Use “I,” not “you always”
      —
      If it escalates, activate the Cool-Down Timer in Tools
      —
      The goal is understanding, not winning
      ⓘ
      These prompts are structured conversation tools, not therapy. Find a couples therapist →

      Resources
      +
      Beyond the categories
      When you need
      more support.
      FUSE Align is not therapy, medical advice, or crisis care. It is a relationship intelligence and conversation tool. The resources below are educational — starting points for finding licensed professionals, not substitutes for them. If you are in crisis, call 988 or 911 immediately.
      In crisis — call or text now
      988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
      Free, confidential, 24/7. Call or text 988 from anywhere in the US for mental health crisis support — for yourself or someone you love.
      Call or text 988 →
      National Domestic Violence Hotline
      Confidential support 24/7 if you or your partner are experiencing emotional, physical, or sexual abuse. Trained advocates available by phone, text, or chat.
      Call 1-800-799-7233 → Text START to 88788
      If you are in immediate physical danger, call 911.
      Where you are
      Recommendations below are tuned to your current state.
      Recommended for you now
      Couples work
      Couples therapy is not one thing. The modality matters. When you contact a therapist, asking about their training tells you a lot about how they will work with you.
      Research-backed
      The Gottman Method
      Built on 40+ years of observational research. Focused on building friendship, managing conflict, and creating shared meaning. Strong with everyday communication patterns.
      Best for: established couples wanting structured tools and clear protocols.
      Find a Gottman therapist →
      Attachment-based
      Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT)
      Developed by Dr. Sue Johnson. Treats relationships as attachment bonds and addresses the emotional patterns that drive disconnection. Helps partners recognize the cycle they get stuck in.
      Best for: couples who feel emotionally disconnected or fight in repetitive cycles.
      Find an EFT therapist →
      Pre-marital · structured
      PREPARE/ENRICH
      An assessment-based approach used widely in pre-marital counseling. Structured, time-limited, with clear endpoints. The most common framework clergy and pre-marital counselors use.
      Best for: engaged couples preparing for marriage; secular or faith-based.
      Find a PREPARE/ENRICH facilitator →
      For couples at a crossroads
      Discernment Counseling
      A short-term approach (1–5 sessions) for couples uncertain whether to stay together. Not traditional couples therapy — it is a structured decision-making process.
      Best for: one partner leaning out, one leaning in — or both unsure.
      Find a Discernment counselor →
      Individual work
      A lot of the work that shows up in relationships belongs to one of you first. These approaches center the individual.
      Parts work
      Internal Family Systems (IFS)
      Developed by Dr. Richard Schwartz. Works with the different “parts” inside you that get activated in conflict — the protector, the wounded one, the critic. Powerful for understanding why you react the way you do.
      Best for: understanding patterns that show up in every relationship, including this one.
      Find an IFS therapist →
      Attachment-focused
      Attachment-based individual therapy
      Helps you understand how your early relationships shaped how you show up in adult ones — anxious, avoidant, secure, or some mix. Especially useful between relationships.
      Best for: patterns that keep showing up regardless of who you are with.
      Find an attachment specialist →
      Life transitions
      Life-transition specialists
      Therapists who focus on identity shifts: empty nest, divorce, career change, retirement, health events. Different from general therapy — they understand transition as its own kind of work.
      Best for: seasons where who you have been is not who you are becoming.
      Find a transition specialist →
      Specialized support
      Certain areas need specialists, not generalists. If any of the below apply, start there — not with a general couples therapist.
      Substance use & addiction
      SAMHSA National Helpline · AA · NA
      Free, confidential, 24/7 treatment referral and information. Call 1-800-662-4357. For ongoing recovery: Alcoholics Anonymous and Narcotics Anonymous offer free peer-led meetings across the US.
      Call SAMHSA → Find a substance use counselor →
      Grief & loss
      GriefShare · Option B · licensed grief counselors
      Grief is not something to “solve.” GriefShare runs support groups across the country. Option B offers resources for resilience after loss. A licensed grief counselor is often more useful than a general therapist in the first year.
      Find a grief counselor →
      Trauma & PTSD
      EMDR · Somatic Experiencing · trauma-informed specialists
      EMDR is the most-researched approach for trauma. Somatic Experiencing works through the body. Both require specialized training beyond a general license. Ask therapists directly about their training.
      Find a trauma specialist →
      LGBTQ+ affirming
      Inclusive Therapists · NQTTCN
      Inclusive Therapists centers BIPOC, LGBTQIA+, neurodivergent, and disabled therapists. The National Queer and Trans Therapists of Color Network (NQTTCN) offers a specialized directory and mental health fund.
      Find an affirming therapist →
      Culturally specific
      Therapy for Black Girls · Latinx Therapy · Asian MHC · South Asian Therapists
      Finding a therapist who shares cultural context changes what sessions feel like. These directories focus on specific communities and the therapists who understand them.
      Browse directories →
      Financial counseling
      Accredited Financial Counselors (AFCPE)
      Different from financial planners. AFCs work on the behavioral and emotional side of money — debt cycles, couples disagreements, financial trauma. Many offer sliding-scale sessions.
      Find an AFC →
      General directories
      If the specialties above do not match, these are the widest search tools.
      Psychology Today
      The most comprehensive US directory. Search by zip, insurance, modality, and specialty.
      Browse directory →
      Open Path Collective
      Sliding-scale therapy from $40–$80 per session. One-time $65 lifetime membership.
      Browse directory →
      BetterHelp · Talkspace
      Online-only platforms. Faster matching, lower friction. Specialization varies by therapist.
      Compare platforms →
      Signs it’s time to bring in a therapist
      — The same conflict keeps coming back, regardless of the words you use
      — One or both of you feels chronically unheard or unsafe
      — Trust has been broken and you do not know how to repair it
      — Individual mental health issues are affecting the relationship
      — You are stuck on a major decision (children, relocation, separation)
      — You are doing the work in apps and books and it is not enough
      Important · Please read

      FUSE Align is a consumer software product. It is not a medical device, is not licensed to provide medical, psychological, psychiatric, or counseling services, and does not create a doctor-patient, therapist-client, or counselor-client relationship. All content — including category questions, alignment scores, and resource listings — is for general informational and educational purposes only and is not personalized medical, psychological, or legal advice.

      Third-party organizations, hotlines, directories, and therapy modalities referenced in this app are independent of FUSE. FUSE does not endorse, vet, credential, or warrant any individual provider or platform. You are solely responsible for your own due diligence when engaging any service, and FUSE is not liable for any action, inaction, advice, or outcome associated with a third-party provider.

      FUSE Align does not guarantee any particular outcome — including relationship compatibility, reconciliation, or continuation of a relationship. Use of this product does not replace professional care.

      If you are experiencing a medical or mental health emergency, stop using this app and contact 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline), 911, or your local emergency number immediately.

      Cool-Down Timer
      Pause. Breathe. Return.
      20:00
      Step away from the conversation. Both partners take this time independently. When the timer ends, come back with a reflection prompt.
      Upgrade
      Unlock More
      Go deeper.
      Anywhere you are.
      The same subscription works whether you are solo, vetting a new partner, or deepening alignment with an existing one. Your profile is yours — it travels with you. All prices are per individual, each person pays their own subscription.
      Free Tier
      Always free · Per user
      $0
      3 categories matched to your stage · Flexibility ratings · Partner invite included · Each person creates their own account · Priority ranking not included
      À la carte unlocks
      One-time · Per user
      $9.99–$14.99/each
      What you get: Unlock an individual category, permanently. Your answer, priority ranking, flexibility profile, category capstone, and alignment reveal with your partner. Sensitive categories (Addiction, Grief, Trust, Health) are $14.99 — standard categories are $9.99.

      What stays static: Your answer locks at purchase. No re-scoring, no drift tracking, no evolution over time. If you want to update later, you re-answer manually. New categories that launch after your purchase cost extra.

      Best for users unlocking 1–4 specific categories. Owned forever, never expires.
      FUSE Align
      Phase 1 · Available now
      $199/yr
      or $19.99/mo · 12-month commitment
      Everything à la carte includes, plus three ongoing streams:

      → Your answers evolve. Quarterly re-scoring prompts, drift detection when your flexibility shifts, a living evolution timeline of how you have changed. Your answers are not frozen at purchase — they grow with you.

      → The framework grows. Every new category launched during your subscription is unlocked automatically. Early access to beta categories. Life-event-triggered categories (new baby, career change, loss) reach you first.

      → Ongoing intelligence. Weekly check-ins, guided conversation library (new conversations added monthly), therapist export, AI conversation coaching, compatibility trend analysis, shared growth goals, framework capstone.

      Benchmarks against couples therapy ($200/session), not couples apps.
      FUSE Match
      Phase 2 · Coming soon
      $399/yr
      or $39.99/mo · 12-month commitment
      Everything in Align, plus: Alignment-first matching with structural compatibility scores · Pre-scored matches on the core 16 dimensions · Profile-as-currency — your answers travel across matches · Private Circle concierge layer · Per user. Built on a different premise than Hinge or Bumble: you are matched on structural alignment, not photos and prompts.
      Align + Match Bundle
      Best value · Per user
      $449/yr
      saves $149 vs buying separately
      Both products, locked at one annual price. For users who want the foundation and the matching layer together. Bundle pricing available once FUSE Match launches. Align subscribers today get first access and guaranteed bundle pricing.
      A note on subscription continuity
      Your answers always stay yours — downloadable at any time, never lost. What stops on cancellation is the ongoing intelligence: new re-scoring prompts, drift tracking, new categories as they launch, and the AI layer. Your evolution timeline keeps what it has, but stops adding new entries.

      The longer you stay continuously subscribed, the more valuable your profile becomes. A five-year unbroken timeline is an asset you cannot recreate — it captures how you actually changed through real life. Gaps from cancellation are not erased, just unobserved.
      Your Commitment
      12
      After all 16 categories
      “If we choose each other, what are we committing to protect — even when it is inconvenient?”
      This is your single capstone question. It’s not about any one category — it’s about the life you are choosing to build together. You’ll reach this question once both of you have completed all 16 core categories. Answer independently, then share.
      Category Capstone
      03
      Category 03 · Capstone
      Money & Financial Philosophy
      You’ve answered the core question and indicated your flexibility. One more question closes this category — not a comparison, a commitment.
      The capstone question
       
      Your capstone is sealed alongside your category answer. When both of you have completed the capstone, it becomes the closing line of this category’s reveal — a shared promise, side by side.
      30-Day Bridge
      Financial Philosophy
      30-Day
      Bridge Plan.
      Four structured conversations over 30 days. Designed to close the transparency gap your alignment data revealed.
      Your Plan
      ✓
      Week 1 — Name the Gap
      Each partner answers: What does financial transparency mean to me, specifically?
      Week 2 — The Origin Story
      Share where your relationship with money comes from. Family, formative moments, fears.
      Week 3 — Design the System
      Propose how you would structure finances together. Joint, separate, or hybrid?
      Week 4 — Commit to the Agreement
      Write a shared financial agreement in your own words. Not legal — relational.

      Add a moment
      Write a note of appreciation. Upload a photo from a real moment. The book grows when you add, or when the app captures a milestone automatically.
      Share a moment
      Pick a moment, generate a beautifully-formatted share image. If the moment includes both of you, Alex approves before it goes out.
      Milestones & Moments is a FUSE Align subscriber feature. Shared between you and your partner.

      How stage changes work
      Your free 3 categories were locked in when you set your stage at signup. You can update your stage anytime as life evolves — your recommended conversations and next steps will update too. But your free 3 do not re-open. Completed categories always carry forward. When you transition to a new stage, you can unlock its recommended 3 as a stage transition pack at a reduced rate.
      Partner & Connection
      Connected Partner
      A
      Alex
      2 categories complete together
      LINKED
      Answer without a partner
      Your answers are saved and ready when you connect a partner.
      Relationship Structure
      Relationship Network Mode
      Align with multiple partners independently. Each pairing has its own separate scores and threads.
      Off — Standard two-partner mode
      Add another partner
      Each connection is fully independent. Your answers with one partner are never visible to another.
      Subscription
      Free Tier
      3 of 16 categories
      Remove Alex?
      Your answers stay. Only the shared alignment scores and connection with Alex are removed. Your individual responses carry forward to any future alignment.
      Simple Pricing
      The same subscription works whether you are solo, vetting a new partner, or deepening alignment with an existing one. Your profile is yours — it travels with you. All prices are per individual. 3 categories free to start, matched to your relationship stage. No card required.
      Free Tier
      Always free · Per user
      $0
      3 categories matched to your stage · Flexibility ratings · Partner invite included · Each person creates their own account · Priority ranking not included
      À la carte unlocks
      One-time · Per user
      $9.99–$14.99/each
      What you get: Unlock an individual category, permanently. Your answer, priority ranking, flexibility profile, category capstone, and alignment reveal with your partner. Sensitive categories (Addiction, Grief, Trust, Health) are $14.99 — standard categories are $9.99.

      What stays static: Your answer locks at purchase. No re-scoring, no drift tracking, no evolution over time. If you want to update later, you re-answer manually. New categories that launch after your purchase cost extra.

      Best for users unlocking 1–4 specific categories. Owned forever, never expires.
      FUSE Align
      Phase 1 · Available now
      $199/yr
      or $19.99/mo · 12-month commitment
      Everything à la carte includes, plus three ongoing streams:

      → Your answers evolve. Quarterly re-scoring prompts, drift detection when your flexibility shifts, a living evolution timeline of how you have changed. Your answers are not frozen at purchase — they grow with you.

      → The framework grows. Every new category launched during your subscription is unlocked automatically. Early access to beta categories. Life-event-triggered categories (new baby, career change, loss) reach you first.

      → Ongoing intelligence. Weekly check-ins, guided conversation library (new conversations added monthly), therapist export, AI conversation coaching, compatibility trend analysis, shared growth goals, framework capstone.

      Benchmarks against couples therapy ($200/session), not couples apps.
      FUSE Match
      Phase 2 · Coming soon
      $399/yr
      or $39.99/mo · 12-month commitment
      Everything in Align, plus: Alignment-first matching with structural compatibility scores · Pre-scored matches on the core 16 dimensions · Profile-as-currency — your answers travel across matches · Private Circle concierge layer · Per user. Built on a different premise than Hinge or Bumble: you are matched on structural alignment, not photos and prompts.
      Align + Match Bundle
      Best value · Per user
      $449/yr
      saves $149 vs buying separately
      Both products, locked at one annual price. For users who want the foundation and the matching layer together. Bundle pricing available once FUSE Match launches. Align subscribers today get first access and guaranteed bundle pricing.
      A note on subscription continuity
      Your answers always stay yours — downloadable at any time, never lost. What stops on cancellation is the ongoing intelligence: new re-scoring prompts, drift tracking, new categories as they launch, and the AI layer. Your evolution timeline keeps what it has, but stops adding new entries.

      The longer you stay continuously subscribed, the more valuable your profile becomes. A five-year unbroken timeline is an asset you cannot recreate — it captures how you actually changed through real life. Gaps from cancellation are not erased, just unobserved.
